text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver for your names is the one-two punch of AI funding momentum (MGX's $49B raise) and analyst calls saying semis aren't topping yet. That's a tailwind for your entire book, but you're on margin so don't let optimism numb you to risk.

Your holdings: All show HOLD status with modest gains - nothing screaming danger. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged semi ETF - decay is real and margin amplifies any gap-down hard. SPAL is brand-new, very volatile, leveraged, and still finding its footing at +1.91% - smallest position discipline matters here. Everything else looks fine today.

Watch: (1) Whether semis hold their intraday trend - SMH is your cleaner read. (2) Oil wobble from Iran talks - indirect but risk-off oil moves can drag broad sentiment fast.

Bottom line: Your book is green and the tape is friendly, but two leveraged ETFs on margin means one bad gap costs you real money - know your stops cold.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - wide but it's leveraged. If SMH shows any intraday reversal today, consider tightening your mental stop or trimming size to protect margin account.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, moves fast. Hard stop $21.38 is your line - no exceptions on margin. No reason to add here yet.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist but no single name has a clean catalyst + pattern combo compelling enough to add margin exposure into.
